name: improving-components
description: Expert at automatically applying improvements to Claude Code components based on quality analysis. Enhances descriptions, optimizes tool permissions, strengthens security, and improves usability. Works in conjunction with analyzing-component-quality skill.
capabilities: Enhances component descriptions for clarity and specificity with auto-invoke triggers
Optimizes tool permissions by removing unnecessary access while maintaining functionality
Strengthens security through input validation requirements and minimal permission principles
Improves documentation structure with examples, capabilities lists, and usage guidelines
Applies systematic improvements based on quality analysis results with before/after validation
Preserves component functionality while upgrading to current best practices
author: C0ntr0lledCha0s
securitySummary: All 108 static findings are false positives. The weak cryptographic algorithm detections are triggered by markdown file extensions and YAML examples in documentation. Ruby backtick execution warnings are markdown code formatting in example snippets. The network reference is legitimate GitHub source metadata. This skill only uses Read and Edit tools for legitimate component improvement purposes. No actual security risks identified.
valueStatement: This skill systematically improves Claude Code components by enhancing descriptions, optimizing tool permissions, strengthening security, and improving usability. It transforms quality analysis results into concrete enhancements while preserving component functionality.
userTitle: Enhance Claude Code Components with Quality Improvements
limitations: Requires quality analysis results to guide improvements and cannot analyze components independently
Only uses Read and Edit tools and cannot execute scripts or perform complex transformations
Focuses on incremental improvements and does not redesign components from scratch
Cannot modify components that have syntax errors preventing file reading

useCases.title: Optimize Tool Permissions for Security
useCases.description: Security engineers can use this skill to review and minimize tool permissions in components, removing unnecessary Bash, Write, or Edit access while maintaining required functionality.

useCases.title: Enhance Component Marketplace Readiness
useCases.description: Component authors can prepare skills and agents for marketplace publication by improving descriptions, adding examples, and meeting quality standards systematically.

useCases.title: Upgrade Legacy Components to Best Practices
useCases.description: Development teams can modernize existing components by applying current documentation standards, security patterns, and usability improvements automatically.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Component Enhancement
promptTemplates.prompt: Improve the description and documentation for my component
promptTemplates.scenario: User has a component with vague description and wants to enhance clarity

promptTemplates.title: Security Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Review and optimize tool permissions for my skill to minimize security risks
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to reduce unnecessary permissions in a component

promptTemplates.title: Marketplace Preparation
promptTemplates.prompt: Prepare my component for marketplace publication by applying all recommended improvements
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to meet quality standards before publishing a component

promptTemplates.title: Systematic Quality Upgrade
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply quality analysis recommendations to improve my component while preserving functionality
promptTemplates.scenario: User has quality analysis results and wants to apply improvements systematically

bestPractices: Always validate that improvements preserve component functionality before applying changes
Prioritize security improvements such as removing unnecessary Bash access or adding input validation requirements
Use quality analysis results to guide evidence-based improvements rather than making arbitrary changes
antiPatterns: Changing what a component does rather than improving how well it does it
Removing tool permissions without verifying they are truly unnecessary for functionality
Applying improvements without showing before and after comparison or quality score validation

faq.question: Will this skill modify the behavior of my components?
faq.answer: No. This skill preserves component intent and functionality while only improving clarity, security, and usability. Changes focus on descriptions, permissions, and documentation.

faq.question: How does this skill work with the analyzing-component-quality skill?
faq.answer: The analyzing skill evaluates components and identifies improvement opportunities. This improving skill takes those findings and applies concrete enhancements systematically.

faq.question: Can this skill fix broken components with syntax errors?
faq.answer: No. This skill requires readable components to analyze and improve. Syntax errors must be fixed manually before applying systematic improvements.

faq.question: What types of components can this skill improve?
faq.answer: This skill works with all Claude Code component types including agents, skills, commands, and hooks that use YAML frontmatter and markdown structure.

faq.question: How do I verify that improvements actually increase quality?
faq.answer: The skill provides before and after quality scores and shows specific changes applied. You can re-run quality analysis to validate improvements objectively.

faq.question: Does this skill require specific tools to be enabled?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill requires Read and Edit tools to analyze component files and apply improvements. No other tools are needed or used.
